title: Rain Collection
desc: Enchanted rainwater is falling. Every drop is precious.

steps.text:
In Everrain, it never stops raining. That's the point — the region is a permanent rainfall, Lake Ken-I-Po's waters seeping through the ceiling of the UnderWorld above in an endless drizzle.

But once a season, the rain changes. It thickens. Glows faintly. Mugic reagent — raw elemental energy dissolved in water. An OverWorld collection team has set up rows of crystal basins, and they need every hand they can get.

"Each basin fills in about ten minutes," the team leader explains. "Pour it into the sealed jugs before it evaporates. Don't spill it. Don't drink it. And if it starts singing, pour it faster."

options.label: Ask what happens if you drink it

steps.text:
The work is simple but relentless. Pour, seal, replace. Pour, seal, replace. The rain tastes like static electricity on your lips. The basins fill faster than you expected — the enchanted rain falls heavier than normal water.

After an hour, you've filled thirty-two jugs. Your arms ache. Your clothes are soaked through with water that tingles against your skin.

Then the rain starts singing. A faint, crystalline hum rises from the basins. The team leader snaps her fingers. "Faster! When it sings, it's at peak concentration. We've got maybe five minutes."

You pour like your life depends on it. Eleven more jugs. Then the singing stops, the glow fades, and it's just rain again.

"Forty-three jugs," the leader counts. "That'll keep the muges supplied for months. Good work."

"Last person who drank it hiccuped sparks for a week." The team leader gives you a look that suggests this is not a joke. "Before that, someone drank a full cup and their hair turned blue. Permanently. She says she likes it now, but she didn't at first."

You decide not to drink it.

The collection goes smoothly — pour, seal, replace, repeat. The enchanted rain feels alive in the basins, swirling in patterns that almost look deliberate. When a basin overflows, the spilled water crawls across the ground like mercury before evaporating.

You fill twenty-eight jugs before the rain returns to normal. Not the best haul, but the leader nods. "Solid. And you didn't drink any. That puts you ahead of most first-timers."

options.label: Leave with dry-ish clothes and no sparking hiccups
